steps.title: Find the base side
steps.content:
Base area equals total surface area minus lateral surface area:
$$
42{,}120-25{,}220=16{,}900
$$
Since the base is a square,
$$
s^2=16{,}900
$$
$$
s=130
$$

steps.title: Find the slant height
steps.content:
The combined area of the four triangular lateral faces is $2s\ell$. Substitute $s=130$:
$$
2(130)\ell=25{,}220
$$
$$
\ell=97
$$

steps.title: Use the right triangle
steps.content: The pyramid height $h$, half the base side $65$, and slant height $97$ form a right triangle: $$ h^2+65^2=97^2 $$ $$ h^2=9409-4225=5184 $$ $$ h=72 $$ The answer is A.
